Title: **Re: CMOS/ADTG/Digic register investigation on ISO**  
Post by: **Levas** on **May 04, 2020, 09:24:07 PM**

Was messing with ADTG register override module and found some interesting registers for video mode (Not sure if these were mentioned before  ???).  
2 registers bump the gain, without dynamic range penalty!  
*Edit: False alarm, the registers seemed to drop frames per second, from 25 to about 8 fps, so while ML was reporting 25fps based on timer registers, the recordings were in 8fps, so more light hitting the sensor...*  
combined they give about 1.66 stop of extra light, without sacrificing dynamic range, works on all iso's.  
Not sure what the trickery is done in the background, but **rolling shutter becomes huge with these 2 registers override**.  
  
(https://live.staticflickr.com/65535/49856218182\_467ca1a7d2\_o.png)  
  
Now comes the weirdness, the registers only become visible in ADTG register override module, after a photo or video is taken.  
To further investigate, I made a crop\_rec preset with these two registers overriden, but they don't get activated the first time.  
Crop\_rec preset is loaded, nothing happens, record one video and after that the settings kick in, **why doesn't it work the first time without having to record a video first ?**  
  
Also tried to take photos with these settings, but photos are messed up, purple or green color, sometimes even messed up frame, timing issues or something.  
  
I don't know how values look on other cams, but I lowered the most left digit of these values until live view becomes brighter.  
  
(https://live.staticflickr.com/65535/49856088831\_972684b6aa\_o.png)  
  
Side by side comparison, same ISO, same Shuttertime and same Diaphragm Aperture  ;). Left with ADTG2 registers 23 and 24 overrides, on the right, basic values (with 1.66 exposure correction in post).  
  
Here are the two dng frames on google drive:  
https://drive.google.com/open?id=1mwtcX7FVyrJTth-Xjqpr0-cdIJxJj9XE (https://drive.google.com/open?id=1mwtcX7FVyrJTth-Xjqpr0-cdIJxJj9XE)  
https://drive.google.com/open?id=11MEW5mOHFBXrBlrssz7uTj0FJx5-Xzj6 (https://drive.google.com/open?id=11MEW5mOHFBXrBlrssz7uTj0FJx5-Xzj6)  
  
  
  
  

Title: **Re: CMOS/ADTG/Digic register investigation on ISO**  
Post by: **a1ex** on **May 05, 2020, 04:12:20 PM**

> [Quote from: Levas on May 04, 2020, 09:24:07 PM](https://web.archive.org/web/20250903081912/https://www.magiclantern.fm/forum/index.php?topic=10111.msg226799#msg226799)  
> 2 registers bump the gain, without dynamic range penalty!  
> combined they give about 1.66 stop of extra light, without sacrificing dynamic range, works on all iso's.  
> Not sure what the trickery is done in the background, but **rolling shutter becomes huge with these 2 registers override**.  

  
Confirmed on 5D3 - really interesting. It appears to reduce the frame rate as well.  
  
ADTG\[23\]: default 0xF00, setting to 0x200 or some other magic values will cut the frame rate in half\*, doubling the exposure time.  
ADTG\[24\]: default 0x2d1e, setting to 0x1d1e will cut the frame rate in half\*, doubling the exposure time.  
\* changing both registers together will result in 1/3 of the original frame rate (e.g. 24 -> 8 fps), and 3x exposure time.  
  
This could explain the results, as the dynamic range does not change significantly with shutter speed.  
  
In addition, changing any of those 2 registers in photo mode appears to have no effect (possibly because of the mechanical shutter), but changing both of them together results in garbled image (possibly timing issues because of slower readout). With FRSP, changing any or both of those 2 registers results in garbled image (?!)  
  
Definitely worth a closer look, in particular at higher ISOs sorry, false alarm :)

Title: **Re: CMOS/ADTG/Digic register investigation on ISO**  
Post by: **megamasterbloc** on **July 08, 2025, 02:22:52 PM**

  
  
This thread hasn't been active for a few years, nevertheless I think my results will be appreciated. While I like shooting videos, I'm mostly a photographer, so getting more dynamic range out of my sensor is greatly appreciated.  
*For those who might care about it, I'm running danne's crop rec from feb 2023 on a 5DIII 1.2.3.*  
  
One of the main issue of the whole ADTG experiment is that results are scattered across the thousands of messages, so a lot of work was put into searching the useful resources from this thread. Another issue is that the module download page got nuked after the recent changes, but I was lucky to have saved the necessary ones (adtg\_gui, iso\_regs and raw\_diag) them just a few days before. If there is the need to, I can share the modules myself.  
  
*note : for the context of this post, ISO means the ISO selected in the canon menu, with eventual tweaks applied to it. I do not claim to understand the mathematics behind computing the effective ISO nor do I have the lab equipment necessary to accurately measure it. For example, if I'm talking about pulled ISO 200, I am talking about the specific configuration that derived from the base ISO 200 configuration.*  
  
In total, I've read this entire thread 4 times. The first two times I barely understood anything, and I couldn't get the modules to work out. The third time I managed to get the modules to work, and began my experiments, mostly focusing on the ADTG side, and got some promising results. The fourth time I read it is where I began understanding CMOS patches, and what all of the registers did. Also, I am now only using atdg\_gui and not iso\_regs due to issues I will describe later.  
  
For now I'm gonna start with my results from tweaking ISO 200 as it's where most of the gains can be found.  
\- ADTG2/4\[fe\] : Works best at 0x3 from 0x4, any lower is already too much, nothing new afaik.  
\- ADTG2/4\[8/9/a/b\] : Works best at 0x0 (on my specific camera, on some you will need to set it at 0x1 pr 0x2 to avoid artefacts) from 0x34/0x33/0x35/0x34/0x32/0x34/0x33. You might be able to fine tune these but the DR gain should be negligible.  
\- ADTG2/4\[888x\] : At sweet spot (around 0x38x) so that WL doesn't drop too much (personally as long as it's above 14500 I consider that it's good as it allow to recover some DR thanks to non linearity). It should be noted that the sweet spot is per register, so it benefit from fine tuning (especially to reduce banding in the highlights). However, the sweet spot tend to change quite a bit when the amp warm up, so just setting it once and for all as some sort of generic value (in my case 0x38d) is probably the least worst thing to do. *In the future I should try to put it in my freezer to see how low I can get.*  
\- CMOS\[4\] : the original CMOS patch, some say it work best at 0x318 or 0x308.  

Code Select Expand

`CMOS[4] 0x300   0x308   0x30b   0x30e   0x310   0x318   DR      11.862  11.873  11.872  11.875  11.879  11.869           11.859  11.867  11.869  11.869  11.869  11.868                   11.871  11.871          11.871   `The sweet spot is between  0x308 and 0x310, you might be able to fine tune it to get 0.01 DR, but is quite inconsistent when warming up  
\- CMOS\[0\] : I've found that 0x884 work better than 0x114 which is consistent with #356 and #503, seems to squeeze another 0.005-0.008 DR.  

Code Select Expand

`0x113  11.860  11.860  11.857  11.860   0x883  11.868  11.867  11.861  11.868   `The last digit seems to have some kind of effect but that is below my reproducibility error, all I know is that 0 will result in a black image.  
\- CMOS\[3\] : This is supposed to only have an effect during high ISO, default at ISO 200 is 0x944, lowering the first digit will reduce DR but increasing it seems to increase the DR, however that is once again below my reproducibility error.  

Code Select Expand

`CMOS[3]  0x944  0xb44  0xd44  0xf44   DR       11.866 11.870 11.865 11.870            11.875 11.874 11.865 11.870   `I haven't tried changing the last two digits.  
  
In total I can consistently get 11.870 DR, I believe that you can get 11.900 with good ADTG gains, but that is quite harder to get due to temperature drift. I've also got some lucky shots with more than 1.880 DR although I'm not sure why. I've also noticed that the increased digital gain in aperture wider than f/4.0 will give another 0.05-0.1 DR but it's also possible that it's just due to the faked WL. I also believe that the rest of the DR until 12 EV can be recovered using non linearity as well as careful sensor calibration and correction. It's also possible that by tweaking digital gain, saturate offset and BW offset we can get 0.01-0.03 more DR but as those registers arn't in adtg\_gui I can't verify. I've also read that ADTG\[7\] might also affect gain but this one is also not in the module.  
  
at f/2.8 and at f/4  
(https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1122091514382782494/1392117798003675157/f2.8.png?ex=686e5e3b&is=686d0cbb&hm=7f27559253e705ebe37577a4d36b9c3fd1d47fe5894400cd34f1ea12913c7b77&) (https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1122091514382782494/1392117797667995678/f4.png?ex=686e5e3b&is=686d0cbb&hm=6cd47556f511c2b36d608b413eb6302b381c6ef1e05ac1ef8c7e04293439176a&)  
  
As for the pro and con of each module :  
\- adtg\_gui : Due to it's nature, the registers are not hardcoded and are discovered upon each restart, this mean that using it for anything other than experiments is not viable. Another issue is that certain registers (digital gain, saturate offset, BW offset, ADTG\[7\]) are not present. Otherwise this is the best module for experimentation.  
\- iso\_regs : it's 5dIII only, doesn't have all of the CMOS registers and all of the ADTG registers are grouped together (in practice that is not a major issue), however the major issue is that for some reason you can't fine tune the values, you only get specific values (like 600, 800, 1000). This is most likely a bug and was already mentioned by someone else, thought I can't fine their message.  
   
My next experiments will focus on ISO 400 to 6400, and when that is done, ISO 100. I'd also like to play with sensor calibration however I'm not sure of the state of this project.

Title: **Re: CMOS/ADTG/Digic register investigation on ISO**  
Post by: **megamasterbloc** on **July 13, 2025, 10:09:56 AM**

That is definitely weird, I've not been able to access those registers however it seems that the source code does include them. It's also entirely possible that by using a newer build those registers somehow don't get discovered, but it's strange because it works fine with iso\_regs.  
If someone with a 5DIII with a recent build is willing to try to get adtg\_gui to show these registers, ask me for the module since it's not available anymore.  
Also I've been experimenting with video, most modes work but anamorphic won't, I'm guessing because it already change a lot of registers.
